notes: EPI wraps openags/paper-search-mcp as an external dependency.
EPI discovery calls search_papers and preserves source coverage telemetry in run reports, including source capabilities, provider readiness, source routing, and provider gaps when available.
EPI demotes unstable sources such as Google Scholar by default and records missing provider env, such as Unpaywall email, as provider gaps rather than paper unavailability.

EPI acquisition tries download_with_fallback first; if no direct PDF URL exists and OA fallback finds no PDF, it records manual-download-required with DOI/publisher links instead of running weak fallback loops.
EPI verifies downloaded PDF identity with lightweight DOI/title checks before MinerU; clear mismatches are quarantined under _epi/quarantine/papers/<slug>/paper.pdf.
When a direct PDF candidate exists, EPI may continue to source-native MCP, CLI, or direct URL fallback; Sci-Hub is disabled unless EPI_PAPER_SEARCH_MCP_USE_SCIHUB is explicitly enabled.
After successful acquisition, EPI may call read_<source>_paper or CLI read to write a non-authoritative retrieval preview sidecar at paper-search-read-preview.txt; this is not replacing MinerU.
Use EPI_PAPER_SEARCH_MCP_COMMAND and EPI_PAPER_SEARCH_MCP_ARGS to override the stdio server command; use EPI_PAPER_SEARCH_COMMAND for the CLI fallback.

doctor --json reports paper_search_provider_readiness so missing Unpaywall email, CORE key, Semantic Scholar key, Google Scholar proxy, DOAJ key, or Zenodo token can be treated as recall/fallback risk rather than hidden failure.
